name: config-hmcl
description: How to read and modify native HMCL launcher configuration that has NO dedicated tool action Рђћ Java path pinning, game directories, launcher theme/background, proxy, window size, auto-join server Рђћ by editing HMCL's JSON config files directly (not via the instance/game domain tools). Instance memory, JVM/GC args, and game options DO have dedicated tool actions (instance(action="set_memory"/"set_jvm_args"/"set_option")) Рђћ use those instead, never raw-edit those three. Use this skill when the user asks to change Java path, window size, auto-join server, game directory, launcher theme/background, or proxy.
body:
# Configuring native HMCL

HMCL stores settings as schema-versioned JSON files. For the settings this skill actually
covers below (the ones with no dedicated tool action), use `read`/`edit`/`write` directly on
the files Рђћ raw file editing, not the `instance` tool. Keep each file's `"$schema"` key intact
or HMCL discards the file as invalid.

## Do NOT raw-edit these three Рђћ dedicated tools exist

- Memory Рєњ `instance(action="set_memory", maxMemoryMB)`.
- JVM/GC args Рєњ `instance(action="set_jvm_args", jvmArgs)`.
- Game options (`options.txt`) Рєњ `instance(action="set_option", key, value)`.

These exist precisely so `instance-game-settings.json` is never hand-edited for them.
`HMCLGameRepository` caches every LOADED instance's game settings in memory and does a
full-file overwrite the next time ANY OTHER property on that same instance changes (memory,
isolation, a mod toggle, Рђд). A raw file write never updates that in-memory copy, so a
hand-edited value for one of these three can be silently clobbered Рђћ with no error, no
warning Рђћ the very next time something unrelated changes for that instance. The dedicated
tool actions mutate the SAME in-memory object HMCL itself holds, so they don't have this
problem. If asked to change one of these three, call the tool action, report the
previous/new value it returns, and stop Рђћ do not also touch the JSON file "just in case".

## Critical: in-memory settings, not just exit-time rewrite

HMCL holds `launcher-settings.json` / `game-settings.json` in memory and **overwrites them on
exit** Рђћ edit those only while HMCL is closed (or via the UI), then tell the user to reopen it.
`instance-game-settings.json` is ALSO cached in memory for every loaded instance (see above),
so it is only safe to raw-edit for fields with no dedicated tool action (Java path, window
size, etc.) Рђћ and even then, tell the user a restart/relaunch of that instance is needed
before the change applies; do not assume editing it live is risk-free.

## File locations (`<.hmcl>` = `<launch dir>/.hmcl`)

| Concern | File |
|---|---|
| Launcher (theme, proxy, language, background, download) | `<.hmcl>/config/launcher-settings.json` |
| User settings (Java list, log retention) | `%APPDATA%/.hmcl/config/user-settings.json` |
| Game directories / profiles | `<.hmcl>/config/game-directories.json` |
| Game settings presets ("global") | `<.hmcl>/config/game-settings.json` (`presets` array) |
| Per-instance overrides | `<gameDir>/versions/<ver>/.hmcl/config/instance-game-settings.json` |

Memory (`maxMemory`) and JVM args (`jvmOptions`) live in the last two files too, but are listed
here for reading/reference only Рђћ never write them directly; use the tool actions above.

## overrideProperties gotcha

`instance-game-settings.json` only applies a field if its name is ALSO listed in
`"overrideProperties"` Рђћ otherwise it silently inherits from `"parent"`. Set BOTH the value
AND add the key to `overrideProperties`, e.g. `"windowType":"FULLSCREEN"` alone does nothing.
To change a setting globally, edit the matching preset in `game-settings.json` instead (no
`overrideProperties` needed there).

## After any change

State which file(s) changed, whether it needs HMCL closed/reopened or just an instance
relaunch, and never fabricate account credentials or IDs.
